tools.description: Passive subdomain discovery tool. Finds valid subdomains using online sources like crt.sh, SecurityTrails, and more.
In-depth attack surface mapping. OWASP tool for network asset discovery using OSINT and active techniques.
Fast subdomain finder from Tomnomnom. Finds domains related to a target from various sources.
Fast and multi-purpose HTTP toolkit. Probes for alive hosts, tech stack, status codes, and more.
Fast port scanner written in Go. Designed for reliability and speed with SYN/TCP/CONNECT scan modes.
High-performance DNS stub resolver. Resolves millions of domains per second for bulk operations.

tools.description: Network exploration and security auditing utility. Port scanning, service detection, OS fingerprinting, and NSE scripts.
Fast and customizable vulnerability scanner based on YAML templates. Community-driven template library.
Fast web fuzzer written in Go. Directory/file discovery, vhost discovery, and parameter fuzzing.
Web path brute-force scanner. Python-based directory and file enumeration tool.

Fast and flexible online password brute-forcing tool. Supports numerous protocols.

tools.description: Advanced password recovery tool. GPU-accelerated hash cracking with support for 300+ hash types.
John the Ripper password cracker. Supports many hash formats and attack modes.
Command-line tool for transferring data with URL syntax. Essential for HTTP testing and API interaction.
Command-line JSON processor. Essential for parsing JSON output from security tools.
Append lines to a file only if they don't already exist. Perfect for deduplication in pipelines.
Send notifications to multiple platforms (Discord, Slack, Telegram, etc.) from pipelines.
Pattern matching utility. Essential for filtering and searching output from security tools.
Sort lines of text. Essential for organizing output from security tools.
Remove duplicate adjacent lines. Usually used after sort for deduplication.
Web application security testing platform. Proxy, scanner, intruder, repeater, and more.
OWASP Zed Attack Proxy. Free, open-source web application security scanner.

linux.command: sudo apt-get install -y zaproxy && pip3 install zapcli

darwin.command: brew install --cask owasp-zap && pip3 install zapcli

tools.description: CTF exploitation framework. Python library for exploit development, binary exploitation, and crypto challenges.
Penetration testing framework. Exploit development, payload generation, post-exploitation, and pivoting.
Active Directory attack path mapping. Identifies privilege escalation paths and trust relationships.

linux.command: pip3 install bloodhound && sudo apt-get install -y bloodhound

darwin.command: pip3 install bloodhound && brew install bloodhound

tools.description: Post-exploitation lateral movement tool. SMB, WinRM, MSSQL, and Active Directory enumeration.
LLMNR/NBT-NS/mDNS poisoner. Captures NTLM hashes from network traffic.
Network protocols implementation. SMB, Kerberos, MSRPC, WMI, DCE/RPC for lateral movement.
Memory forensics framework. Analyzes RAM dumps for malware, artifacts, and incident response.
Network protocol analyzer. Deep packet inspection, traffic analysis, and protocol decoding.
Pattern matching for malware detection. Signature-based malware identification.
Digital forensics platform. Disk image analysis, file recovery, timeline generation.
Firmware analysis and embedded file extraction. Identifies and extracts files from binary images including file systems, compression artifacts, and bootloaders.
File carving tool for recovering deleted or hidden files from disk images. Recovers files based on headers, footers, and internal data structures.

win32.command: Use WSL: sudo apt-get install -y foremost

tools.description: Fast digital forensics tool that extracts features from disk images without parsing the file system. Extracts emails, URLs, credit cards, phone numbers, and more.
Metadata extraction from files including images, audio, video, and documents. Essential for OSINT and forensic file analysis.

flags.name: -G
flags.description: Show group name for each tag

tools.description: Command-line network packet analyzer. Captures and analyzes network traffic. Essential for network forensics and protocol analysis.
Wireshark's command-line interface. Powerful PCAP analysis with protocol dissection, field extraction, and filtering. Scriptable alternative to Wireshark GUI.
NSA's open-source reverse engineering framework. Disassembly, decompilation, scripting, and binary analysis for multiple architectures including x86, ARM, MIPS, and more.
Open-source portable reverse engineering framework. Disassembly, debugging, hex editing, binary diffing, and scripting via r2pipe. Supports 40+ architectures.

linux.command: git clone https://github.com/radareorg/radare2 && cd radare2 && sys/install.sh

tools.description: GNU Project Debugger. Full-featured debugger for C/C++, assembly, and other compiled languages. Core tool for dynamic binary analysis and exploit development.
GDB plugin for exploit development. Provides enhanced commands for heap analysis, ROP gadget search, memory visualization, and CTF/pwn challenge solving on top of GDB.

win32.command: Use WSL: git clone https://github.com/pwndbg/pwndbg && cd pwndbg && ./setup.sh

tools.description: Library call tracer. Intercepts and records dynamic library calls made by an executing process. Useful for reversing obfuscated binaries and understanding program behavior.

flags.name: -r
flags.description: Show time relative to previous call

darwin.command: Not available on macOS — use dtruss or dtrace

win32.command: Use WSL: sudo apt-get install -y ltrace

tools.description: System call tracer. Intercepts all system calls made by a process. Essential for understanding binary I/O behavior, file access, and privilege escalation analysis.

darwin.command: Not available on macOS — use dtruss or dtrace

win32.command: Use WSL: sudo apt-get install -y strace

tools.description: Python binary analysis framework. Symbolic execution, concolic testing, CFG recovery, and automated vulnerability discovery. Used for binary CTF challenges and security research.
Free and open-source GUI for radare2. Provides a visual interface for reverse engineering including disassembly view, decompiler (Ghidra plugin), graph view, and scripting.
Android APK reverse engineering tool. Decompiles APK resources to near-original form, rebuilds APKs, and handles resource decoding (resources.arsc, XML manifests).
DEX to Java decompiler. Produces Java source code from Android DEX and APK files. Includes a GUI (jadx-gui) for browsing decompiled code, searching strings, and cross-referencing.
Dynamic instrumentation toolkit for Android, iOS, macOS, Windows, and Linux. Hook native functions, trace APIs, dump memory, bypass SSL pinning, and patch at runtime without source code.

Runtime mobile exploration toolkit built on Frida. Automates common mobile pentest tasks: SSL pinning bypass, root/jailbreak detection bypass, memory dumping, file system exploration, and class introspection.

flags.name: -g
flags.description: Gadget or bundle ID to target

tools.description: Mobile Security Framework — automated all-in-one mobile application security assessment tool. Static and dynamic analysis for Android APK/AAB and iOS IPA. REST API for CI/CD integration.
Android Debug Bridge — command-line interface for communicating with Android devices. Essential for mobile pentest: install/pull APKs, logcat, shell access, port forwarding, and device management.

flags.name: -s
flags.description: Direct command to specific device serial

flags.name: -d
flags.description: Direct command to only USB-connected device

tools.description: Ultra-fast TCP port scanner. Scans the entire internet in under 6 minutes. Transmit-only design with custom TCP/IP stack for maximum speed.
Modern port scanner written in Rust. Scans all 65k ports in 3 seconds then feeds results to nmap for service detection.
OSINT tool for gathering emails, subdomains, hosts, employee names, open ports from public sources like search engines and DNS.
Subdomain enumeration tool using search engines (Google, Bing, Yahoo, DNSDumpster, VirusTotal) and brute-force.
Automated OSINT framework with 200+ modules. Correlates data from DNS, WHOIS, social media, threat intelligence, and more.
Find credentials and secrets in git repos, S3 buckets, and filesystems. Verifies secrets against APIs to eliminate false positives.
SAST tool for detecting hardcoded secrets (API keys, passwords, tokens) in git repos. Fast and configurable with built-in and custom rules.
Hunt down social media accounts by username across 400+ platforms including Twitter, GitHub, Instagram, LinkedIn and more.
Check if an email address is registered on 120+ online platforms including Twitter, Instagram, GitHub and more.
Collect user information by username from 3000+ sites. Profiles social network footprint with detailed metadata extraction.
Fast and configurable web crawler and spider from ProjectDiscovery. Supports headless mode, JS rendering, and scope control.
HTTP parameter discovery suite. Finds hidden GET/POST parameters using wordlists and multi-threaded probing.
Interactive man-in-the-middle HTTP/HTTPS proxy. Intercept, inspect, modify, and replay web traffic. Scriptable via Python addons.
Free command-line tool to check SSL/TLS encryption. Tests ciphers, protocols, vulnerabilities (BEAST, POODLE, Heartbleed) and certificate issues.
Domain name permutation engine to detect typosquatting, phishing, and corporate espionage. Generates and checks variations of domain names.
MITM attack framework for phishing credentials while bypassing 2FA. Proxies target website to capture session cookies.
Social Engineering Toolkit. Comprehensive suite for social engineering attacks: spear-phishing, website cloning, credential harvesting, and payload delivery.

darwin.command: git clone https://github.com/trustedsec/social-engineer-toolkit.git && cd social-engineer-toolkit && pip3 install -r requirements.txt

tools.description: Network execution tool — successor to CrackMapExec for SMB, WinRM, LDAP, MSSQL, SSH. Mass credential testing and lateral movement automation.
Tool for enumerating and exploiting Active Directory Certificate Services (AD CS). Finds vulnerable certificate templates for privilege escalation.
Kerberos brute-force and user enumeration tool. Performs AS-REP roasting, password spraying, and user enumeration against Active Directory.
WinRM shell for pentesting Windows targets. Supports pass-the-hash, pass-the-ticket, Kerberos authentication, file upload/download, and PowerShell remoting.
Fast TCP/UDP tunnel over HTTP, secured with SSH. Enables port forwarding and SOCKS proxy through firewalls and restricted networks.
Advanced tunneling and pivoting tool using TUN interfaces. No SOCKS proxy needed — agents create real network interfaces for seamless pivoting.
Open-source cross-platform C2 framework by BishopFox. Generates implants, manages sessions, and supports MTLS/HTTP/DNS/WireGuard C2 channels.
Modern C2 framework with an advanced GUI and extensible shellcode loaders. Supports multiple agents and post-exploitation modules.
Collaborative multi-user C2 framework with a web UI. Plugin-based architecture supporting multiple agents (Athena, Apollo, Merlin) and C2 profiles.
Privilege Escalation Awesome Scripts (LinPEAS/WinPEAS). Automated local privilege escalation enumeration for Linux, Windows, and macOS.
Netcat replacement with persistence-focused post-exploitation. Handles shell stabilization, file upload/download, and automated enumeration on connection.
Exploitation framework for embedded devices and routers. Modules for exploiting CVEs in Cisco, Huawei, D-Link, TP-Link, and other network devices.

tools.description: Automated WiFi phishing framework. Creates rogue access points with realistic captive portals to capture WPA credentials via social engineering.
Swiss army knife for network attacks and monitoring. ARP spoofing, DNS spoofing, HTTP/HTTPS MITM, WiFi scanning, BLE scanning, and scriptable modules.
Capture WiFi packets and PMKID hashes from WPA/WPA2 networks. Designed for offline WPA password cracking with hcxtools and hashcat.
Steganography tool for hiding data in JPEG, BMP, WAV, and AU files. Password-protected embedding and extraction of secret messages.
Steganography brute-force tool for uncovering hidden data in image files. Supports steghide and other steganography tools.
Python tool for reverse engineering Android applications. APK parsing, DEX disassembly, control flow analysis, and taint analysis.
All-in-one security scanner for containers, filesystems, git repos, and cloud configs. Detects CVEs, misconfigurations, secrets, and generates SBOMs.
Security assessment tool for AWS, GCP, and Azure. Performs 300+ checks for CIS benchmarks, GDPR, HIPAA, and custom compliance frameworks.
AWS exploitation framework. Post-exploitation and persistence in AWS environments — privilege escalation, data exfiltration, and lateral movement.
Multi-cloud security auditing tool. Gathers data from AWS, GCP, Azure, Alibaba Cloud, and Oracle Cloud to identify misconfigurations and risks.
Monitor Linux processes without root privileges. Sniffs process creations by watching /proc. Useful for detecting cron jobs, privilege escalation vectors, and scheduled tasks.
Common User Passwords Profiler. Generates targeted password lists based on personal information gathered through social engineering or OSINT.
